reasoning: As of late August 2025, Russia remains approximately 30-40 km away from Sloviansk. Between Russian forces and Sloviansk lies heavily fortified territory and other major objectives, including Pokrovsk and Kostiantynivka, which are currently the primary focus of Russian offensives. Sloviansk is part of the main "fortress belt" in Donetsk Oblast. The pace of the Russian battlefield advance—even though it reached its fastest rate since 2024 in August—does not suggest the capacity to cover tens of kilometers and actually seize control of a major fortified city within the span of 4 months. Furthermore, the highly-anticipated Trump-Putin summit on August 15, 2025, wrapped up without an agreement on a ceasefire or negotiated settlement, removing the primary non-military path to capturing the city this year. The resolution criteria explicitly require actual control, not just a de jure agreement. Therefore, the chance of Russia securing actual military control of the Sloviansk train station by December 31, 2025, is exceedingly low. The Polymarket price of 24% on August 21 appears significantly overvalued compared to the on-the-ground reality, though it keeps me from dropping the probability below 5-6% to account for unknown tail risks such as a sudden frontline collapse.
